resources.string: Forward incoming SMS to your Slack channel
How to create a Slack webhook in 2 minutes
Connect SMS to your Slack channel

This app listens for incoming SMS messages and triggers a Slack Workflow via webhook — automatically sending the message data to your Slack channel.\n\nNo server required. Set up a Workflow in Slack, paste the webhook URL here, and you\'re done.

sender +886912345678
message Your verification code is 1234.
receiver +886987654321
device Pixel 7
timestamp 2026-04-11T08:30:00Z
In Slack, click your workspace name → Tools → Workflow Builder. Click "New Workflow".
Select "Starts from a webhook" as the trigger. Slack will generate a unique webhook URL.
Add 5 Text variables named exactly: sender, message, receiver, device, timestamp.
Add a step — Send a message
Click "Add Step" → "Send a message". Choose a channel and compose your message using the variables, e.g. "📱 SMS from {{sender}}: {{message}}".
Click "Publish". Copy the webhook URL (starts with https://hooks.slack.com/triggers/…) and paste it into this app.
Sends a sample SMS to Slack to verify your webhook is working.
Pick a real SMS from your inbox and preview exactly how it will look in Slack before going live.
Please enter a Slack webhook URL
SMS permission required to forward messages
Phone permission denied — receiver number will be empty
